candidates.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: Kendall tau is also invariant to mean subtraction (contrastive scoring), just like rank-based metrics. Mean subtraction shifts all scores for a given ref column by the same constant, preserving ranks. Kendall tau measures rank concordance, so it is equally invariant. FM2 detection via contrastive scoring requires a metric that is sensitive to score magnitudes, not just ranks. The contamination injection experiment (p1_fm2_contamination_injection) with non-uniform contamination is the key test. NEW FINDINGS: (1) DiagIF achieves 0.9819 AUPRC on toxicity (best) and 0.9647 R@50 on counterfact (matching RepSim), suggesting diagonal Fisher inverse is highly effective. (2) BM25 achieves perfect R@50=1.0 on counterfact at pilot scale. (3) RawDotIF achieves 0.9542 AUPRC on toxicity, confirming gradient norm as primary toxicity signal.
